raw_consumer_statement:
Barclays closed my stolen package dispute even though I provided all required information. Now theyre requiring me to send a physical mail to reopen the case, which is an unfair burden. I never received the goods and should not be held liable under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Barclays Zero Liability Policy.

I purchased goods from XXXX  XXXX which was delivered by a transportation company whos known to have bad customer service and delivery service. The package was not left in a safe location or place to the side, it was just thrown on the step. A total of XXXX items were stolen amounting {$150.00}. The merchant did not help, didnt offer to investigate or even replace my items. I reached out to the delivery company XXXX and they did nothing but said the photo showed delivered. Nothing whatsoever to help. Now Barclays telling me to pay for something I didnt receive. Apparently they closed the case because I didnt respond. Why would you need to call to ask if I got the package, why would I get it and not say anything or waste my precious time. I had all the stuff for my XXXX XXXX and this process has been so inconvenient and frustrating. I didnt mail when I opened the claim so why am I being asked to go out of my way to mail something when Im already frustrated.
